title: Frank's Back in Business

jokes.text: Bring in... the warthog.
jokes.setup: Serious business discussion about saving the company with 'one man'
jokes.punchline: Referring to Frank as 'the warthog' - an animalistic nickname
jokes.explanation: Strong character introduction with perfect economy - the nickname 'warthog' immediately establishes Frank's reputation and the show's tone

dialogue.line: Bring in... the warthog.

jokes.text: Hey, Frank, how do you know that these are crow's eggs? Because I saw a crow.
jokes.setup: Charlie asking for scientific verification of bird identification
jokes.punchline: Frank's hilariously simple and useless logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank moment - confident delivery of completely inadequate reasoning. Strong character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Hey, Frank, how do you know that these are crow's eggs?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Because I saw a crow.

jokes.text: Oh, boom, 24 hours is up. The found wallet is ours. Let's pop this bad boy open and see what we've won. Mr. Brian LeFeve from Quebec, you lose.
jokes.setup: Finding a wallet and waiting 24 hours
jokes.punchline: Treating it like winning a prize and taunting the victim
jokes.explanation: Establishes the gang's amorality perfectly - they're genuinely excited about stealing

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, boom, 24 hours is up. The found wallet is ours. Let's pop this bad boy open and see what we've won. Mr. Brian LeFeve from Quebec, you lose.

jokes.text: Oh, shit. Is that Canadian? Ah, throw it in the trash. I'm not gonna throw it in the trash. Might be worth more than ours.
jokes.setup: Finding Canadian money in the wallet
jokes.punchline: Mac's immediate dismissal vs Dennis's greedy consideration
jokes.explanation: Good character differentiation but fairly standard ignorance humor

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, shit. Is that Canadian? Ah, throw it in the trash.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I'm not gonna throw it in the trash. Might be worth more than ours.

jokes.text: Charlie can't read. He'll adapt. He'll adapt to reading?
jokes.setup: Frank claiming Charlie will adapt to business
jokes.punchline: The fundamental impossibility of 'adapting' to literacy
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure with Mac's incredulous response. Excellent character integration with Charlie's illiteracy.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Charlie can't read. He'll adapt. He'll adapt to reading?

jokes.text: What are you gonna do if he comes looking for it? Uh, tell him to suck a boner.
jokes.setup: Legitimate concern about wallet owner confronting them
jokes.punchline: Mac's childishly aggressive non-solution
jokes.explanation: Classic Mac bravado with childish phrasing. Good character work.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: What are you gonna do if he comes looking for it?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Uh, tell him to suck a boner.

jokes.text: Free shit, free shit. Jesus Christ. Act like you belong here.
jokes.setup: Gang discovers luxury box amenities
jokes.punchline: Mac and Dee acting like children while Dennis tries to maintain composure
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast between Dennis trying to be sophisticated and others being obvious frauds

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Free shit, free shit. Jesus Christ. Act like you belong here.

jokes.text: All right, I'll run the op. This the only entrance and/or exit, therefore if somebody comes in, I will spring off the balcony to safety.
jokes.setup: Need for a realistic exit strategy if caught
jokes.punchline: Mac's ridiculous 'tactical' plan involving jumping off a balcony
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac delusion - treating their petty crime like a military operation

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: All right, I'll run the op. This the only entrance and/or exit, therefore if somebody comes in, I will spring off the balcony to safety.

jokes.text: Dennis, if I had a gun with me, I'd be spraying bullets into the air as I fell.
jokes.setup: Mac's already ridiculous balcony escape plan
jokes.punchline: Adding completely unnecessary gunfire to a 50-foot fall
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Mac's delusion. The visual of shooting while falling is perfectly absurd and quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Dennis, if I had a gun with me, I'd be spraying bullets into the air as I fell.

jokes.text: Oh, uh, yeah, yeah, hi... It's nice to finally put a face to the name.
jokes.setup: Dennis caught off guard when asked if he's Brian LeFeve
jokes.punchline: His awkward confirmation and the businessman's response suggesting they know each other
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the impersonation plotline, cringe factor as Dennis is in over his head

dialogue.line: Oh, uh, yeah, yeah, hi... It's nice to finally put a face to the name.

jokes.text: Vic Vinegar, bodyguard. I don't shake, so don't even try.
jokes.setup: Introduction to business associates
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous fake name and unnecessarily aggressive stance
jokes.explanation: Excellent fake name and Mac's typical overcommitment to a role. Very quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Vic Vinegar, bodyguard. I don't shake, so don't even try.

jokes.text: Also, there are too many minorities and women working here. What's up with that?
jokes.setup: Frank giving business directives to Charlie
jokes.punchline: Casually dropping in blatant discrimination as a business concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank characterization - casual bigotry delivered as business advice

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Also, there are too many minorities and women working here. What's up with that?

jokes.text: Let me ask you a question. Did I just do your job for you? Uh, I-I guess so. You're fired.
jokes.setup: Frank helping fix a printer jam
jokes.punchline: Immediately firing the employee for accepting help
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from helpful gesture to firing. Shows Frank's twisted business logic.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Let me ask you a question. Did I just do your job for you?

dialogue.speaker: Employee
dialogue.line: Uh, I-I guess so.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: You're fired.

jokes.text: I seen better-looking moose turds in Rick Moranis' backyard, ya hosers.
jokes.setup: Dee pretending to be Canadian commenting on a business proposal
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top Canadian stereotype insult with random celebrity reference
jokes.explanation: Decent stereotype humor but the Rick Moranis reference feels forced

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I seen better-looking moose turds in Rick Moranis' backyard, ya hosers.

jokes.text: This is about the thrill of wearing another man's skin. Feeling his innermost wants and desires and being in control of his every single move.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining why he's committed to impersonating Brian LeFeve
jokes.punchline: Describing identity theft in deeply disturbing, sexual terms
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dennis characterization - taking something mundane and making it psychopathic. Highly quotable and disturbing.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: This is about the thrill of wearing another man's skin. Feeling his innermost wants and desires and being in control of his every single move.

jokes.text: That's how you get off. Now don't you guys want to get off with me?
jokes.setup: Dennis's sexual language about identity theft
jokes.punchline: Innocent phrase made sexual through context
jokes.explanation: Perfect double entendre setup that becomes a running gag. Dennis's obliviousness makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: That's how you get off. Now don't you guys want to get off with me?

jokes.text: Charlie, Charlie, Charlie, we don't build anything. Leave that to the Chinks.
jokes.setup: Charlie asking what the company actually makes
jokes.punchline: Frank's casual racism and dismissal of actual production
jokes.explanation: Character-consistent but the slur feels more shock than wit

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Charlie, Charlie, Charlie, we don't build anything. Leave that to the Chinks.

jokes.text: No, I want to eat sushi off of some Jap broad's tits.
jokes.setup: Charlie asking if Frank wants an expensive sushi dinner
jokes.punchline: Frank's grotesquely specific racist and sexist demand
jokes.explanation: Pure shock humor without much craft. Character-consistent but lazy writing.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: No, I want to eat sushi off of some Jap broad's tits.

jokes.text: Um, bodyguards. What about bodyguards? Training bodyguards, putting them in a facility and... What about them? Oh, what about a resort?
jokes.setup: Mac trying to pitch a business idea
jokes.punchline: Complete inability to articulate any coherent concept
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac incompetence - all confidence, no substance. Good cringe comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Um, bodyguards. What about bodyguards? Training bodyguards, putting them in a facility and... What about them? Oh, what about a resort?

jokes.text: I was told there'd just be one, but I can take both of you. Might get a little sore, but I'll manage.
jokes.setup: Dennis and Mac meeting what they think is a child
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's an adult masseuse with sexual undertones they misunderstood
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - audience thinks like Mac/Dennis that something inappropriate is happening

dialogue.speaker: Masseuse
dialogue.line: I was told there'd just be one, but I can take both of you. Might get a little sore, but I'll manage.

jokes.text: That's your goddamn problem, pal, you don't know your limits 'cause you're not willing to push yourself. I know my limits! This is my limit.
jokes.setup: Dennis criticizing Mac for not pushing boundaries
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately proving the point by stating his limit
jokes.explanation: Good ironic structure with Mac immediately contradicting Dennis's point about knowing limits

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: That's your goddamn problem, pal, you don't know your limits 'cause you're not willing to push yourself.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I know my limits! This is my limit.

jokes.text: Brian LeFeve. Who? Brian LeFeve. The wallet they found? Holy shit!
jokes.setup: Charlie revealing who the controlling shareholder is
jokes.punchline: The revelation that they've been impersonating the very person Frank needs
jokes.explanation: Excellent plot convergence that pays off the episode's setup. Great structural comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Brian LeFeve. Who? Brian LeFeve.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: The wallet they found?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Holy shit!

jokes.text: What are you talking broken English for? I thought they could understand me better if I...
jokes.setup: Charlie speaking to Japanese businessman in broken English
jokes.punchline: His racist assumption about communication
jokes.explanation: Standard ignorance humor, character-consistent for Charlie but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What are you talking broken English for? I thought they could understand me better if I...

jokes.text: You bring us a great honor to join with us, and we have many a naked woman and the fish of pleasure for you...
jokes.setup: Charlie greeting Japanese businessman
jokes.punchline: Horribly offensive broken English stereotype
jokes.explanation: Shows Charlie's ignorance but relies too heavily on racist stereotypes for humor

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You bring us a great honor to join with us, and we have many a naked woman and the fish of pleasure for you...

jokes.text: There's been an abundance of crow eggs all around Philly. And crows seem to be getting bigger. By the year and the day, even.
jokes.setup: Business meeting requiring professional presentation
jokes.punchline: Charlie's obsession with crow eggs presented as business intelligence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - treating his weird obsession as valuable market research. Great callback.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: There's been an abundance of crow eggs all around Philly. And crows seem to be getting bigger. By the year and the day, even.

jokes.text: You're fired... You keep talking about products and quality and making things... You're out.
jokes.setup: Charlie trying to contribute ideas about actual productivity
jokes.punchline: Frank firing him specifically for caring about creating things
jokes.explanation: Good satire of corporate culture where actually caring about the work is seen as a liability

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: You're fired... You keep talking about products and quality and making things... You're out.

jokes.text: Fight Milk! The first alcoholic, dairy-based protein drink for bodyguards... By bodyguards!
jokes.setup: Professional business presentation to investors
jokes.punchline: Completely ridiculous product with impossible contradictions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist product that perfectly captures the gang's incompetence. Highly quotable and memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Fight Milk! The first alcoholic, dairy-based protein drink for bodyguards... By bodyguards!

jokes.text: I drink it every morning so I can fight like the crow.
jokes.setup: Product testimonial for Fight Milk
jokes.punchline: Comparing fighting ability to a scavenger bird
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of Charlie's crow fixation. Absurd logic delivered with complete sincerity.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I drink it every morning so I can fight like the crow.

jokes.text: Well, that's not Brian LeFeve! This is Brian LeFeve.
jokes.setup: Mac reveals Dennis has been impersonating someone
jokes.punchline: He produces what appears to be the real Brian LeFeve
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic revelation that sets up the gruesome punchline to come.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Well, that's not Brian LeFeve! This is Brian LeFeve.

jokes.text: This guy here... He's like, 'Dude, get a fingerprint.' And I heard, 'Get a fingertip.' And I chopped his finger off really quick
jokes.setup: Explaining why he has a severed finger
jokes.punchline: Mishearing 'fingerprint' as 'fingertip' and acting on it immediately
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that's perfectly in character. The casual way Charlie describes mutilation is peak Sunny.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: This guy here... He's like, 'Dude, get a fingerprint.' And I heard, 'Get a fingertip.' And I chopped his finger off really quick

jokes.text: Just a couple of people who totally got off, bro.
jokes.setup: Businessmen asking who Dennis and Dee really are
jokes.punchline: Brings back the 'getting off' theme as Dennis admits the whole thing was for thrills
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to the sexual undertones established earlier. Dennis admits the deception was purely for his own gratification.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Just a couple of people who totally got off, bro.

jokes.text: Just a couple of people who totally got off, bro. You did? Mm-hmm, yeah. Big time, yeah.
jokes.setup: Revealing their true identities to the business associates
jokes.punchline: Confirming they achieved sexual satisfaction from the deception
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that reveals the sexual interpretation was literal all along. Great payoff.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Just a couple of people who totally got off, bro.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You did?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Mm-hmm, yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Big time, yeah.

jokes.text: Some women just don't know how. What?! I don't know what it is. Sometimes it's only the guy who gets off.
jokes.setup: Dee's frustration about not 'getting off' from the con
jokes.punchline: Dennis explaining it like sexual performance issues
jokes.explanation: Great extension of the sexual metaphor that makes Dee's frustration even funnier

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Some women just don't know how.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: What?!

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I don't know what it is. Sometimes it's only the guy who gets off.

jokes.text: Some women just don't know how.
jokes.setup: Dee complaining she couldn't 'get off' from the experience
jokes.punchline: Dennis mansplaining her inability to enjoy deception like sexual dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis condescension treating Dee's lack of enjoyment as a personal failing rather than normal human behavior

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Some women just don't know how.

jokes.text: I stripped the company, sold it to the Chinks, and they shut it down. In a sense, you're all fired.
jokes.setup: Frank's dramatic speech about loving the company
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal revealing he sold and destroyed it for money
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank moment - goes from sentimental to ruthlessly capitalistic in seconds. Classic bait and switch.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I stripped the company, sold it to the Chinks, and they shut it down. In a sense, you're all fired.

jokes.text: I stripped the company, sold it to the Chinks, and they shut it down. In a sense, you're all fired.
jokes.setup: Frank's passionate speech about loving the company
jokes.punchline: Immediately revealing he destroyed it for profit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank twist - undermining his emotional speech with pure greed. Great structural payoff.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I stripped the company, sold it to the Chinks, and they shut it down. In a sense, you're all fired.

jokes.text: I thought you loved the company. I do. But not more than money.
jokes.setup: Charlie's confusion about Frank's contradictory actions
jokes.punchline: Frank's honest admission about his priorities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank logic delivered with complete honesty. The economy is excellent.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I thought you loved the company.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I do. But not more than money.

jokes.text: I do. But not more than money.
jokes.setup: Charlie asking if Frank loves the company
jokes.punchline: Frank's perfectly honest admission about his priorities
jokes.explanation: Perfectly encapsulates Frank's character - brutally honest about his greed with perfect comic timing and economy

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I do. But not more than money.

jokes.text: Speaking of which, tell me more about this Crowtein.
jokes.setup: Frank just destroyed a company for money
jokes.punchline: Immediately shows interest in Mac and Charlie's ridiculous crow-based product
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending callback that shows Frank's opportunistic nature and validates the ridiculous Fight Milk concept

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Speaking of which, tell me more about this Crowtein.

tmdb_overview: With Charlie under his wing, Frank cleans up so he can get his hands dirty and help out his old company with one last big merger. An unwritten rule of the bar gets the rest of the gang caught up in a case of mistaken identity.
